blocks:
Ayahs

ٱلْحَآقَّةُ (۱)
The Besieger! (1)
مَا ٱلْحَآقَّةُ (۲)
What is the Besieger?! (2)
وَمَآ أَدْرَىٰكَ مَا ٱلْحَآقَّةُ (۳)
What will show you what is the Besieger?! (3)
كَذَّبَتْ ثَمُودُ وَعَادٌۢ بِٱلْقَارِعَةِ (٤)
Thamudand ‘Ad denied the Cataclysm. (4)
فَأَمَّا ثَمُودُ فَأُهْلِكُوا۟ بِٱلطَّاغِيَةِ (٥)
As for Thamud, they were destroyed by the Cry. (5)

Key Points

Al-Haaqqah (The Inevitable Reality) derives from haqq, meaning a fixed and established matter. This word is one of the names of the Day of Judgment (Qiyamah), because Qiyamah is a certain, true, and fixed event. Al-Qaari'ah (The Striking Calamity) derives from qar', meaning to strike or pound. This means that the world, on the verge of the establishment of Qiyamah, will face a striking incident. Three means for the destruction of the people of Thamud are mentioned in the Quran: a) Earthquake. «فأخذتهم الرجفة»(1) b) Cry/Shout. «انّا أرسلنا علیهم صیحة»(2) c) Thunderbolt. «مثل صاعقة عاد و ثمود»(3) Perhaps all three punishments occurred together and were inseparable—meaning thunder, lightning, and earthquake—or perhaps each punishment destroyed a different group of people. It is necessary to create sensitivity, motivation, and eagerness to hear important matters. The repetition of the word Al-Haaqqah, and addressing the Prophet personally—who is the noblest of creation—all serve to create this sensitivity.

1) A'raf, 78. 2) Qamar, 31. 3) Fussilat, 13.

Messages

1- The Day of Judgment is an extremely great and terrifying day. «الحاقّة ما الحاقّة»

2- The Day of Judgment cannot be known except through revelation. «ما أدراك ما الحاقة»

3- Those who deny the Day of Judgment face Divine wrath in this world. «كذّبت ثمود و عاد بالقارعة... فاهلكوا»

4- The Day of Judgment is a striking and terrifying incident. «القارعة»

5- Not all punishments occur on the Day of Judgment; the punishment of some nations occurs right here in this world. «فاهلكوا بالطّاغیة»

6- Denial is the prelude to transgression, and transgression is the cause of destruction. «كذّبت... فاهلكوا بالطّاغیة»

7- Characteristics and behaviors are the criteria for receiving good or evil. «بالطاغیة»

Explanations

Portrait of Surah Al-Haaqqah This Surah has fifty-two verses and was revealed in Mecca. The name of the Surah is taken from the first verse and means a fixed and established matter. The central theme of this Surah is the Day of Judgment and its characteristics, and three names of the Day of Judgment—Al-Haaqqah, Al-Qaari'ah, and Al-Waaqi'ah—are mentioned in this Surah. The unfortunate fate of previous nations, the greatness of the Quran and the Prophet, and the characteristics of Hell and Paradise, along with their inhabitants, form the subsequent sections of this Surah.

Ayahs

وَأَمَّا عَادٌ فَأُهْلِكُوا۟ بِرِيحٍ صَرْصَرٍ عَاتِيَةٍ (٦)
And as for ‘Ad, they were destroyed by a fierce icy gale, (6)
سَخَّرَهَا عَلَيْهِمْ سَبْعَ لَيَالٍ وَثَمَـٰنِيَةَ أَيَّامٍ حُسُومًا فَتَرَى ٱلْقَوْمَ فِيهَا صَرْعَىٰ كَأَنَّهُمْ أَعْجَازُ نَخْلٍ خَاوِيَةٍ (۷)
which He clamped on them for seven gruelling nights and eight days, so that you could see the people there lying about prostrate as if they were hollow trunks of palm trees. (7)
فَهَلْ تَرَىٰ لَهُم مِّنۢ بَاقِيَةٍ (۸)
So do you see any remaining trace of them? (8)

Key Points

Ṣarṣar (صَرصَر) means a cold and harsh wind, and ‘Ātiyah (عاتیة) is derived from ‘Utu (عُتو) meaning rebellious or tyrannical. Ḥusūm (حسوم) is the plural of Ḥāsim (حاسم), meaning the repeated and continuous performance of an action until complete severance and destruction. Khāwiyah (خاویة) means both hollow and something that has fallen to the ground. Ṣar‘ā (صرعى), the plural of Ṣarī‘ (صریع), refers to something that has fallen onto the ground, and A‘jāz (أعجاز), the plural of ‘Ajuz (عَجُز), means the trunk of a tree. In Ayah 20 of Sūrat Al-Qamar, we read: «كانّهم اعجاز نخل منقعر», meaning the destruction of the people of ‘Ād was such that it was as if the tall trunks of date palms had been uprooted.

Messages

1- Allah’s hand is open regarding the type of punishment. He destroys the people of Thamūd with the fire of lightning and the people of ‘Ād with a cold wind. «بریح صرصر»

2- The effects of cosmic phenomena are in Allah’s hand. Wind sometimes causes ships and clouds to move and rain to descend, and sometimes it is a means of destruction. «بریح صرصر»

3- Divine punishments are sometimes sudden and sometimes gradual. «سبع لیال و ثمانیة ایّام»

4- Bulk and strength do not prevent destruction. Even if a person’s stature and height are like a date palm, they fall to the ground before Divine wrath. «اعجاز نخل خاویة»

5- Punishments are not uniform; sometimes they are completely obliterating: «فجعلهم كعصف مأكول», but sometimes the body remains. «اعجاز نخل خاویة»

Ayahs

وَجَآءَ فِرْعَوْنُ وَمَن قَبْلَهُۥ وَٱلْمُؤْتَفِكَـٰتُ بِٱلْخَاطِئَةِ (۹)
Then Pharaoh and those who were before him, and the towns that were overturned, brought about iniquity. (9)
فَعَصَوْا۟ رَسُولَ رَبِّهِمْ فَأَخَذَهُمْ أَخْذَةً رَّابِيَةً (۱٠)
They disobeyed the apostle of their Lord, so He seized them with a terrible seizing. (10)
إِنَّا لَمَّا طَغَا ٱلْمَآءُ حَمَلْنَـٰكُمْ فِى ٱلْجَارِيَةِ (۱۱)
Indeed when the Flood rose high, We carried you in a floating ark, (11)
لِنَجْعَلَهَا لَكُمْ تَذْكِرَةً وَتَعِيَهَآ أُذُنٌ وَٰعِيَةٌ (۱۲)
that We might make it a reminder for you, and that receptive ears might remember it. (12)

Key Points

«Mu'tafikāt» is the plural of «Mu'tafikah», derived from «I'tifāk», meaning to be turned upside down. It refers to the regions of the people of Lot. «Rābiyah» is derived from Ribā, meaning increasing growth. ‘Adhāb Rābiyah (a severe punishment) means a harsh punishment. «Ta‘īhā» is derived from «Wa‘y», meaning preservation, understanding, and acceptance. Zamakhshari, Fakhr Razi, Maraghi, and Qurtubi, among the Sunni commentators, and Shaykh Tabarsi, Abu al-Futuh Razi, and Allamah Tabataba'i, among the Shi'a commentators, have narrated traditions according to which «Udhunun Wā‘iyah» (an attentive ear) is applied to Hadrat Ali عليه السلام. The singular form of the word «Udhun» (ear) confirms this point: that the one who preserved all the truths was only one ear.

Messages

1- The people of Lot were in several regions, and all of them were destroyed. «Mu'tafikāt»

2- Obedience to the Prophets is obligatory, and opposing them leads to destruction. «Fa ‘aṣaw rasūla rabbihim fa-akhadhnāhum akhdhata rābiyah»

3- Disobedience to the Prophets also incurs worldly punishment. «Fa ‘aṣaw... fa-akhadhahum»

4- Allah completes the argument (proof); first, He sends a Messenger, and if the people disobey, He seizes them with His wrath. «Fa ‘aṣaw rasūl... fa-akhadhahum akhdhata rābiyah»

5- Divine wrath is severe. «Akhdhatan rābiyah»

6- Amidst wrath and punishment, Allah shows kindness to a group. «Lammā ṭaghā al-mā’u ḥamalnākum fī al-jāriyah»

7- The people of the Arabian Peninsula are descendants of the survivors of Noah. «Ḥamalnākum»

8- Lessons must be learned from bitter events. «Li-naj‘alahā lakum tadhkirah»

9- Preserving relics, history, and words is a value. «Ta‘īhā udhunun wā‘iyah»

10- What is heard is valuable only if it is accompanied by understanding and preservation. «Ta‘īhā udhunun wā‘iyah»

Ayahs

فَإِذَا نُفِخَ فِى ٱلصُّورِ نَفْخَةٌ وَٰحِدَةٌ (۱۳)
When the Trumpet is blown with a single blast (13)
وَحُمِلَتِ ٱلْأَرْضُ وَٱلْجِبَالُ فَدُكَّتَا دَكَّةً وَٰحِدَةً (۱٤)
and the earth and the mountains are lifted and levelled with a single levelling, (14)
فَيَوْمَئِذٍ وَقَعَتِ ٱلْوَاقِعَةُ (۱٥)
then, on that day, will the Imminent [Hour] befall (15)
وَٱنشَقَّتِ ٱلسَّمَآءُ فَهِىَ يَوْمَئِذٍ وَاهِيَةٌ (۱٦)
and the sky will be split open—for it will be frail on that day— (16)
وَٱلْمَلَكُ عَلَىٰٓ أَرْجَآئِهَا ۚ وَيَحْمِلُ عَرْشَ رَبِّكَ فَوْقَهُمْ يَوْمَئِذٍ ثَمَـٰنِيَةٌ (۱۷)
with the angels all over it, and the Throne of your Lord will be borne that day by eight [angels]. (17)
يَوْمَئِذٍ تُعْرَضُونَ لَا تَخْفَىٰ مِنكُمْ خَافِيَةٌ (۱۸)
That day you will be presented [before your Lord]: none of your secrets will remain hidden. (18)

Key Points

Dakk (دَكّ) means to crush and destroy, and it is also used for smooth and soft ground. Wāhiyah (واهیة) means weak (or flimsy), and Arjā’ (أرجاء) means sides and regions. The repetition of the word Yawma’idhin (یومئذٍ) in these verses is a sign of the importance of the Day of Resurrection (Qiyamah). Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The eight individuals who bear the Throne (Arsh), some of them are Prophets and some are Imams: "Noah, Abraham, Moses, Jesus, Muhammad, Ali, Hasan, and Husayn." And the meaning of the Throne (Arsh) is Divine Knowledge. Therefore, on the Day of Resurrection, above the angels (fawqahum), are the friends of Allah (Awliyā’ Allāh) who know the deeds of the people. (4) Imam Ali عليه السلام said: The Divine Throne (Arsh) is not a seat, but rather a limited and created being, subject to management, which the angels are commanded to carry with the power they possess from Allah. (5)

4) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 5) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- The blowing of the Trumpet (Sūr) is an inevitable matter. «اذا نفخ فى الصور»

2- The disintegration of the existing order occurs through a thunderous sound. «نفخ فى الصّور... فدكّتا»

3- The earth will be thrown out of its orbit, the mountains will be uprooted from their places, and they will be crushed together in a single collision. «و حملت الارض و الجبال فدكّتا»

4- The collapse of the mountains will be with intensity and speed. «دكّة واحدة»

5- The important event is the Day of Resurrection (Qiyamah), and every other event is insignificant compared to it. «وقعت الواقعة»

6- The end of the firm heaven, «سبعاً شداداً» (seven strong ones), is weakness. «فهى یومئذٍ واهیة»

7- Although the existing order will be disrupted on the Day of Resurrection, another order will prevail—an order established by the presence of angels. «و الملك على ارجائها و یحمل عرش ربّك فوقهم ثمانیة»

8- All hidden dimensions of human personality will be displayed on the Day of Resurrection, and the true personality of man—goodness and evil, thoughts and motives, flaws and perfections—will be revealed and presented. «لا تخفى منكم خافیة»

Ayahs

فَأَمَّا مَنْ أُوتِىَ كِتَـٰبَهُۥ بِيَمِينِهِۦ فَيَقُولُ هَآؤُمُ ٱقْرَءُوا۟ كِتَـٰبِيَهْ (۱۹)
As for him who is given his book in his right hand, he will say, ‘Here, take and read my book! (19)
إِنِّى ظَنَنتُ أَنِّى مُلَـٰقٍ حِسَابِيَهْ (۲٠)
Indeed I knew that I will encounter my account [of deeds].’ (20)
فَهُوَ فِى عِيشَةٍ رَّاضِيَةٍ (۲۱)
So he will have a pleasant life, (21)
فِى جَنَّةٍ عَالِيَةٍ (۲۲)
in an elevated garden, (22)
قُطُوفُهَا دَانِيَةٌ (۲۳)
whose clusters [of fruits] will be within easy reach. (23)
كُلُوا۟ وَٱشْرَبُوا۟ هَنِيٓـًٔۢا بِمَآ أَسْلَفْتُمْ فِى ٱلْأَيَّامِ ٱلْخَالِيَةِ (۲٤)
[He will be told]: ‘Enjoy your food and drink, for what you had sent in advance in past days [for your future life].’ (24)

Key Points

«Ha'ūm» is a noun that carries the meaning of a command, meaning "Come here" (or "Take this"). «Quṭūf», the plural of «Qaṭf», refers to fruit that has been picked or is ready to be picked. «Ẓann» (conjecture/assumption) in worldly matters is a belief accompanied by doubt, but in matters of the Hereafter, it means certainty (or assurance). «Aslaftum», derived from «Islāf», means presenting something with the hope that something better will return in exchange. Every human being will have a scroll (or record) and a file on the Day of Judgment, and the scroll of the righteous will be given into their right hand. Perhaps the group referred to as the Companions of the Right Hand (Aṣḥāb al-Yamīn) in Sūrat al-Wāqi‘ah are these very people. The intent of "highness" (‘uluww) in «fī jannatin ‘āliyah» (in a lofty Garden) may refer to high status, not necessarily high location. On the Day of Judgment, there are both material and spiritual blessings; the contentment of man is a spiritual blessing, and the sublime Paradise is a material blessing. The word «Hanī’an» (pleasantly/wholesomely) from Allah makes the enjoyment greater and more palatable. The word «Hanī’an» has been used four times in the Qur’an, three of which refer to the blessings of Paradise.

Messages

1- On the Day of Judgment, the record of everyone's deeds will be placed in his possession. «ūtiya kitābah»

2- On the Day of Judgment, Allah will judge, punish, and reward people based on a written and documented record. «ūtiya kitābah»

3- Not just certainty, but even the assumption (ẓann) and conjecture regarding the occurrence of the Day of Judgment can control a person. «innī ẓanantu annī mulāqin ḥisābiyah»

4- The Day of Judgment is the time for harvesting the seeds that were sown in the world. «bimā aslaftum fī al-ayyāmi al-khāliyah»

5- The desire to discover one's achievements (or perfections) is an innate matter and persists until the Day of Judgment. «hā'umu iqra'ū kitābiyah»

6- If faith in accountability and reckoning leads to piety, avoidance of unlawful worldly pleasures, and temporary deprivations, then life in Paradise will, in return, be completely satisfying. «fī ‘īshatin rāḍiyah fī jannatin ‘āliyah»

Ayahs

وَأَمَّا مَنْ أُوتِىَ كِتَـٰبَهُۥ بِشِمَالِهِۦ فَيَقُولُ يَـٰلَيْتَنِى لَمْ أُوتَ كِتَـٰبِيَهْ (۲٥)
But as for him who is given his book in his left hand, he will say, ‘I wish I had not been given my book, (25)
وَلَمْ أَدْرِ مَا حِسَابِيَهْ (۲٦)
nor had I ever known what my account is! (26)
يَـٰلَيْتَهَا كَانَتِ ٱلْقَاضِيَةَ (۲۷)
I wish death had been the end of it all! (27)
مَآ أَغْنَىٰ عَنِّى مَالِيَهْ ۜ (۲۸)
My wealth did not avail me. (28)
هَلَكَ عَنِّى سُلْطَـٰنِيَهْ (۲۹)
My authority has left me.’ (29)
خُذُوهُ فَغُلُّوهُ (۳٠)
[The angels will be told:] ‘Seize him, and fetter him! (30)
ثُمَّ ٱلْجَحِيمَ صَلُّوهُ (۳۱)
Then put him into hell. (31)
ثُمَّ فِى سِلْسِلَةٍ ذَرْعُهَا سَبْعُونَ ذِرَاعًا فَٱسْلُكُوهُ (۳۲)
Then bind him in a chain, seventy cubits in length. (32)
إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ لَا يُؤْمِنُ بِٱللَّهِ ٱلْعَظِيمِ (۳۳)
Indeed he had no faith in Allah, the All-supreme, (33)
وَلَا يَحُضُّ عَلَىٰ طَعَامِ ٱلْمِسْكِينِ (۳٤)
and he did not urge the feeding of the needy, (34)

Key Points

Dhira' (ذِراع) is the distance from the elbow to the tip of the fingers, and in the past, it was a standard measure for length. The word "seventy" (هفتاد) is either meant literally or is a metaphor for a long chain. Jahīm (جَحیم), derived from jahmah, refers to a blazing fire. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: If one link of the chain with which the inhabitants of Hell are bound were placed upon the world, the world would melt from the intensity of the heat.(6) In this Ayah, disbelief (kufr), stinginess, and indifference toward the deprived are mentioned side by side. «لا یؤمن باللّه... لا یحضّ» Seizing, binding, dragging, and throwing into Hell indicate the ultimate humiliation and disgrace of the inhabitants of Hell. «خذوه فغلّوه... صلّوه... فاسلكوه»

6) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Comparing the fate of the righteous and the wicked is a Quranic method for better understanding the truths. «اوتى كتابه بیمینه... اوتى كتابه بشماله»

2- In education and guidance, good tidings and warning are necessary side by side. «اوتى كتابه بیمینه... اوتى كتابه بشماله»

3- Awareness of the future is a step toward making correct decisions today. «فیقول یا لیتنى»

4- The window of hope is closed for wrongdoers in the Hereafter. Before seeing the record of deeds, they had hope, but after that, they only feel regret. «یا لیتنى... یا لیتها»

5- Wealth and power will be ineffective on the Day of Judgment. «ما اغنى عنّى مالیه هلك عنّى سلطانیة»

6- Hoarding wealth in the world causes regret on the Day of Judgment. «ما اغنى عنّى مالیه»

7- The punishment for the hands that were open in the world for all kinds of corruption and oppression is the shackles and chains of the Day of Judgment. «فغلّوه»

8- A person who does not have the light of the knowledge of Allah in his heart and service to people in his actions deserves to burn. «لا یؤمن... و لا یحضّ»

9- Attention to the hungry is mentioned alongside faith in Allah. «لا یؤمن... و لایحضّ»

10- Even if we ourselves are unable to help, we must encourage others to assist the hungry. «و لا یحضّ على طعام المسكین»

11- Attending to the poor does not require the poor person to be a believer (mu'min). «و لا یحضّ على طعام المسكین»

Ayahs

فَلَيْسَ لَهُ ٱلْيَوْمَ هَـٰهُنَا حَمِيمٌ (۳٥)
so he has no friend here today, (35)
وَلَا طَعَامٌ إِلَّا مِنْ غِسْلِينٍ (۳٦)
nor any food except pus, (36)
لَّا يَأْكُلُهُۥٓ إِلَّا ٱلْخَـٰطِـُٔونَ (۳۷)
which no one shall eat except the iniquitous.’ (37)
فَلَآ أُقْسِمُ بِمَا تُبْصِرُونَ (۳۸)
I swear by what you see (38)
وَمَا لَا تُبْصِرُونَ (۳۹)
and what you do not see: (39)
إِنَّهُۥ لَقَوْلُ رَسُولٍ كَرِيمٍ (٤٠)
it is indeed the speech of a noble apostle (40)
وَمَا هُوَ بِقَوْلِ شَاعِرٍ ۚ قَلِيلًا مَّا تُؤْمِنُونَ (٤۱)
and it is not the speech of a poet. Little is the faith that you have! (41)
وَلَا بِقَوْلِ كَاهِنٍ ۚ قَلِيلًا مَّا تَذَكَّرُونَ (٤۲)
Nor is it the speech of a soothsayer. Little is the admonition that you take! (42)
تَنزِيلٌ مِّن رَّبِّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (٤۳)
Gradually sent down from the Lord of all the worlds. (43)

Key Points

Ghislīn (غِسلین) means the bloody discharge and pus that flows from the bodies of the inhabitants of Hell. The intent of Rasūl Karīm (رسول كریم - Noble Messenger) is the person of the Prophet of Islam, not Gabriel the Trustworthy. This is because in the subsequent verses we read that He is neither a poet nor a soothsayer, and the accusation of being a poet or soothsayer was directed at the Prophet, not Gabriel. The polytheists considered the Qur'an a book of poetry and called the Prophet a poet, even though not a single poem has been narrated from the Prophet.(7) Punishment on the Day of Judgment is commensurate with the sins committed. Whoever has no compassion for the needy in this world will have no sympathetic friend in the Hereafter: فلیس له الیوم هاهنا حمیم (So he has not here this Day any devoted friend), and whoever did not feed others in this world will be tormented by food on that Day. The greatest oath is found in this Sūrah: فلا اقسم بما تبصرون و ما لا تبصرون (Nay! I swear by what you see, and what you do not see). This means the entirety of existence, the visible and the invisible, which are vastly more numerous than the visible—the world of the unseen, angels, and jinn which are not visible, and the heights of galaxies and the depths of the seas which are not naturally seen. The Qur'an was revealed gradually, because the word tanzīl (تنزیل) denotes gradual revelation. Of course, the entire Qur'an was revealed upon the heart of the Prophet on the Night of Qadr (Power), and then gradually over the course of 23 years. It is like depositing a large sum of money in a bank all at once and then gradually withdrawing it through transfers or checks. The Prophet possesses absolute nobility (karāmah). Rasūl Karīm (رسول كریم - Noble Messenger) implies nobility in dealing with his spouse, enemy, friend, neighbor, and the poor, both in hardship and in joy. In Sūrah Al-Ghāshiyah, verse 6, we read that the food of the inhabitants of Hell is nothing but Ḍarīʿ (ضریع): لیس لهم طعام الاّ من ضریع. And in Sūrah Ad-Dukhān we read: انّ شجرة الزقّوم طعام الاثیم (8) (Indeed, the tree of Zaqqūm is the food of the sinful). And in verse 36 [of this Sūrah] we read: و لا طعام الاّ من غسلین (And no food except Ghislīn). Therefore, either Zaqqūm and Ḍarīʿ are the same, and Ghislīn is their drink—meaning they have one type of food and one type of drink. Or, it is possible that each group of the inhabitants of Hell has a different type of food: one group Ḍarīʿ, one group Zaqqūm, and one group Ghislīn.

7) Tafsīr Al-Mīzān. 8) Ad-Dukhān, 44.

Messages

  1. The inhabitants of Hell are neither spiritually comfortable (لیس له الیوم ههنا حمیم) nor physically comfortable (و لا طعام الاّ من غسلین).

  2. Disbelief in the Great God and indifference towards the hungry is an unforgivable error. لا یأكله الا الخاطئون (None shall eat it except the sinners).

  3. In the Divine perspective, all existence is valuable, as Allah has sworn by it. فلا اقسم بما تبصرون و ما لا تبصرون.

  4. We should not be confined by material things; the unseen are numerous. ما لا تبصرون (what you do not see).

  5. In leadership, defend your upright personnel and dispel the doubts raised against them. و ما هو بقول شاعر... و لا بقول كاهن (Nor is it the word of a poet... nor the word of a soothsayer).

  6. Allah completes the argument (establishes proof). The revelation descends from the Lord of the Worlds, and the intermediary of the revelation is the Noble Messenger. لقول رسول كریم... تنزیل من ربّ العالمین.

  7. The preacher should not expect all or the majority of people to believe. قلیلاً ما تؤمنون (Little is what you believe).

  8. Faith alone is not sufficient; remembrance (dhikr) is also necessary. It is possible to have faith but still be heedless. قلیلاً ما تؤمنون... قلیلاً ما تذكّرون (Little is what you believe... Little is what you remember).

  9. Do not refrain from speaking the truth due to the rejection of the majority. لقول رسول كریم... قلیلاً ما تؤمنون.

Ayahs

وَلَوْ تَقَوَّلَ عَلَيْنَا بَعْضَ ٱلْأَقَاوِيلِ (٤٤)
Had he faked any sayings in Our name, (44)
لَأَخَذْنَا مِنْهُ بِٱلْيَمِينِ (٤٥)
We would have surely seized him by the right hand (45)
ثُمَّ لَقَطَعْنَا مِنْهُ ٱلْوَتِينَ (٤٦)
and then cut off his aorta, (46)
فَمَا مِنكُم مِّنْ أَحَدٍ عَنْهُ حَـٰجِزِينَ (٤۷)
and none of you could have held Us off from him. (47)
وَإِنَّهُۥ لَتَذْكِرَةٌ لِّلْمُتَّقِينَ (٤۸)
Indeed it is a reminder for the Godwary. (48)
وَإِنَّا لَنَعْلَمُ أَنَّ مِنكُم مُّكَذِّبِينَ (٤۹)
Indeed We know that there are some among you who deny [it]. (49)
وَإِنَّهُۥ لَحَسْرَةٌ عَلَى ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ (٥٠)
And indeed it will be a [matter of] regret for the faithless. (50)
وَإِنَّهُۥ لَحَقُّ ٱلْيَقِينِ (٥۱)
It is indeed certain truth. (51)
فَسَبِّحْ بِٱسْمِ رَبِّكَ ٱلْعَظِيمِ (٥۲)
So celebrate the Name of your Lord, the All-supreme. (52)

Key Points

"تَقَوَّل" (taqawwal) means attributing speech to someone who did not say it. "وَتین" (wateen) is the vein that carries blood to the heart, and if it is severed, the person dies. The Quran is a source of regret for the disbelievers in this world and the Hereafter. In this world, they regret that they cannot produce anything like it, and in the Hereafter, they regret why they did not believe in it. Seizing with the "Yameen" (right hand) is a metaphor for power, as the strength of the right hand is greater. Allah has no reservations (or favoritism) with anyone. When Allah speaks to His Prophet in this manner, others must take account of their own actions. The Quran possesses characteristics, including: "تنزیل من ربّ العالمین" (A revelation from the Lord of the worlds), "لتذكرة للمتّقین" (A reminder for the righteous), "لحقّ الیقین" (The absolute certainty). The Prophet has never attributed anything falsely to Allah, because the word "لَو" (if) is used in a context where the action is impossible (or hypothetical/unlikely). In the previous verses, Allah offered the best defense of His Prophet, but at the same time, in this verse, He also issues a threat that if the Prophet were to falsely attribute any speech to God, his life-vein would be severed with power.

Messages

1- Allah is the Guardian of the Revelation, and no one has the power to decrease or increase it. "و لو تقوّل علینا بعض الاقاویل"

2- The Prophet himself, like others, must preserve the sanctity of the Revelation. "و لو تقوّل"

3- All human beings, even the Prophet, are equal before the Divine laws. "و لو تقوّل... لاخذنا منه الوتین"

4- The Quran is not only not the speech of a poet or a soothsayer, but it is also not the speech of the Prophet (Himself). "و لو تقوّل..."

5- Leniency and tolerance are forbidden regarding the law and the Word of Allah. "و لو تقوّل..."

6- Dealing decisively prevents others from coveting control (or interference). "لاَخَذنا منه بالیمین"

7- The Quran enjoys complete immunity (from alteration). "و لو تقوّل"

8- Defending the truth is more important than the individual. For the immunity of the Revelation, We would take the life of even the best of people. "لو تقوّل... لاخذنا..."

9- To preserve the boundaries and sanctity of the Quran, one must speak decisively. "لاخذنا منه بالیمین"

10- Major sins incur major punishments. "لاخذنا منه... ثمّ لقطعنا..."

11- Anything that has greater value must be guarded more strictly. "تنزیل من ربّ العالمین... لو تقوّل... لاخذنا... لقطعنا..."

12- The wrath of Allah upon those who violate the sanctity of the Revelation is destruction. "لقطعنا منه الوتین"

13- No one has the power to offer any resistance against the Divine wrath. "فما منكم من احدٍ عنه حاجزین"

14- The effect of the Quran on the righteous (Muttaqin) is serious and certain. "و انّه لتذكرة للمتّقین"

15- The prerequisite for accepting advice is the spirit of piety (Taqwa). "لتذكرة للمتّقین"

16- Opposing the righteous (Muttaqin) are the deniers (Mukadhdhibin). "للمتّقین... منكم مكذّبین"

17- The preacher should not expect all people to submit to him. "منكم مكذّبین"

18- The deniers should know that they are under the observation of God. "لنعلم انّ منكم مكذّبین"

19- In the face of the stances taken by the deniers, continue your path under the protection of the remembrance of God. "فسبّح باسم ربّك العظیم"

20- Glorifying Allah (Tasbih) is in line with the nurturing of humanity. "فسبّح باسم ربّك العظیم"
